subject: [PLATFORM] Remove certificate generation deployment
commit_message:
[PLATFORM] Remove certificate generation deployment

- remove certificate generation deployment
- change certificate secret for provider (from server to client)
- correct documentation

Issue-ID: OOM-2712
Signed-off-by: Piotr Marcinkiewicz <piotr.marcinkiewicz@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I21a8e277e1fd5f5190c7b67fb6ef97c1b3355230

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Hmmm does it mean that cert-manager will be mandatory for onap deployments with cmpv2 enabled?

If so then shouldn't we document it somewhere?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> Hmmm does it mean that cert-manager will be mandatory for onap deployments with cmpv2 enabled?
> 
> If so then shouldn't we document it somewhere?

Yes it's mandatory, there's a mention of that here: https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_quickstart_guide.html saying:
"If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager."
Should we add something more?

Patch Set 3:

Yes, Cert-Manager is required when CMPv2Enabled=true. Information about this has been added to https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_quickstart_guide.html in Note in step 4. - "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager."
Please check and provide information if I should make more changes to the documentation.

Patch Set 3:

Offline build with default OOM project setup succeeded.

However now that you start discussing CMPv2 I have some doubts: the sentence in the mentioned doc states "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager." - this suggests that by default it's disabled, however I can see that doing "git grep -i cmpv2Enabled" in project tree returns several "cmpv2Enabled: true" which suggests that it's rather _enabled_ by default. 

And by the doc for it to work I'd need Cert-Manager installed which I don't have in my deployment pipeline but the charts still manage to deploy and ONAP is more or less functional.

?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Yes, Cert-Manager is required when CMPv2Enabled=true. Information about this has been added to https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_quickstart_guide.html in Note in step 4. - "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager."
> Please check and provide information if I should make more changes to the documentation.

So when I click on the link I see:
https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_setup_paas.html

which states that ONAP PaaS is optional but it seems that it's no longer true right?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Offline build with default OOM project setup succeeded.
> 
> However now that you start discussing CMPv2 I have some doubts: the sentence in the mentioned doc states "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager." - this suggests that by default it's disabled, however I can see that doing "git grep -i cmpv2Enabled" in project tree returns several "cmpv2Enabled: true" which suggests that it's rather _enabled_ by default. 
> 
> And by the doc for it to work I'd need Cert-Manager installed which I don't have in my deployment pipeline but the charts still manage to deploy and ONAP is more or less functional.
> 
> ?

It is disabled by default. In order to enable it, you should have cmpv2Enabled: true and 
platform: 
  enabled: true
By default platform enabled is set to false and I think that's why your deployment looks well.
In case of running it with cmpv2Enabled: true, platform.enabled: true and without Cert-Manager, at least Cert-Service and SDNC pods shouldn't correctly start.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > Yes, Cert-Manager is required when CMPv2Enabled=true. Information about this has been added to https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_quickstart_guide.html in Note in step 4. - "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager."
> > Please check and provide information if I should make more changes to the documentation.
> 
> So when I click on the link I see:
> https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-oom/en/latest/oom_setup_paas.html
> 
> which states that ONAP PaaS is optional but it seems that it's no longer true right?

It is required only when cmpv2Enabled: true, platform.enabled: true otherwise Cert-Manager is optional.

If "optional" in title is misleading for you, I can remove it.

Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager." - this suggests that by default it's disabled, however I can see that doing "git grep -i cmpv2Enabled" in project tree returns several "cmpv2Enabled: true" which suggests that it's rather _enabled_ by default. 
> > 
> > And by the doc for it to work I'd need Cert-Manager installed which I don't have in my deployment pipeline but the charts still manage to deploy and ONAP is more or less functional.

> It is disabled by default. In order to enable it, you should have cmpv2Enabled: true and 
> platform: 
>   enabled: true
> By default platform enabled is set to false and I think that's why your deployment looks well.
> In case of running it with cmpv2Enabled: true, platform.enabled: true and without Cert-Manager, at least Cert-Service and SDNC pods shouldn't correctly start.


I looked closer at my setup and deployment and indeed sdnc pods didn't initialize:

onap-sdnc-0                                         0/2     Init:0/6     0          15h
onap-sdnc-ansible-server-7d58bfd486-th2kk           0/1     Init:1/2     91         15h
onap-sdnc-dmaap-listener-5548b96879-49dzb           0/1     Init:1/2     0          15h
onap-sdnc-ueb-listener-6fd6fd85d6-6fqdg             0/1     Init:1/2     0          15h
onap-sdnc-web-54895d757c-js54s                      0/1     Init:2/3     90         15h

I'm using onap-all.yaml which has all components enabled (platform including) so in my opinion, in current default setup CMPv2 is ENABLED by default. I believe onap-all.yaml is a de facto default values file used as such by many.

In this context I think the documentation is to vague: there's only a short info about the need to install cert-manager, but no mentions of cmpv2Enabled and platform component needed to be set do "enabled".

My recommendations:
1.Since platform is enabled by default in onap-all.yaml I'd recommend setting cmpv2Enabled to "false" by default.
2.The documentation should be altered - at least it should mention: what is the default setup and that cmpv2Enabled and platform have to be set to "enabled" + cert-manager deployed in order to leverage the cmpv2 compatibility layer in ONAP.

My assumptions above are that your statement "If you want to use CMPv2 certificate onboarding, Cert-Manager must be installed. Click here to see how to install Cert-Manager." means that cert-manager is mandatory for cmpv2 to function properly, and not that the cmpv2 is mandatory for Honolulu at all.

 kubernetes/aaf/values.yaml:  cmpv2Enabled: true

 kubernetes/contrib/values.yaml:  cmpv2Enabled: true

 kubernetes/platform/values.yaml:  cmpv2Enabled: true

To me it is enabled by default so not optional.

Patch Set 4:

A deployment with platform.enabled=true and global.cmpv2Enabled=false succeeded and sdnc pods are up. 

So my recommendation here is to set global.cmpv2Enabled=false by default if we really want this functionality to be optional.
